MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-13-26 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT MADELINE BERG'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF LARRY VAN DEN HEUVEL IS GRANTED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF SEEKING LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT IF AND WHEN IN GOOD FAITH HE IS ABLE TO ALLEGE ADDITIONAL ULTIMATE FACTS SUPPORTING PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THE MERE ALLEGATION THAT DEFENDANT WAS LOOKING AT HER CELL PHONE WHICH CAUSED AN ACCIDENT IS INSUFFICIENT. NO OPPOSITION FILED. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. VISITING JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-23-2026 Set For Trial On Dec-14-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-05-2026 Continued To May-06-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MADELINE BERG MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

Defendant Madeline Berg's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the complaint filed by plaintiff Larry Van Den Heuvel is granted without prejudice to plaintiff seeking leave to file an amended complaint if and when in good faith he is able to allege additional ultimate facts supporting punitive damages. The mere allegation that defendant was looking at her cell phone which caused an accident is insufficient. No opposition filed.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)
